column1: # Curated diff-test corpus: small, fast specs (TLC <10s) covering diverse language features.
# Used by scripts/diff_tlc.sh and the diff-tlc CI workflow.
# Columns (tab-separated):
#   spec_id            - human-readable name (also used in logs)
#   module_path        - path to .tla relative to repo root
#   config_path        - path to .cfg relative to repo root
#   expect_violation   - "yes" if invariant violation expected, "no" otherwise
#   notes              - short description of what the spec exercises
# Lines starting with '#' are comments. Blank lines ignored.
# State counts are *not* pinned here — they are compared at runtime between
# tlaplusplus and TLC, and any mismatch causes the diff harness to fail.
# To add a spec: pick something <10s under TLC, run scripts/diff_tlc.sh locally,
# confirm zero divergences, then commit. If a spec exposes a real divergence,
# leave it OUT of this list and file a bug — see the bottom of this file for
# the running list of "known divergent" specs that we deliberately don't gate on.
simple_counter
column5: tiny baseline; integer arithmetic; no violation

column1: simple_counter_violation
set_operators
record_set
column5: record sets [a: S, b: T] cross-products

column1: negative_int
column5: negative integer arithmetic, branching state space (~10K distinct)

column1: nested_except
recursive
multiple_extends
checkpoint_drain
column5: larger internal model; ~26K distinct states; ~3s under TLC

column1: queue_segment_sync_fixed
column5: regression for T1.1 soundness fix; 1531 distinct states; \E segId \in S : Action(segId) inside disjunctive Next

column1: queue_segment_sync_buggy
column5: regression for T1.1 soundness fix; expected SegmentsRecoverable violation must be caught

column1: view_test
column5: regression for T1.5 splitter soundness fix; 106 distinct under VIEW <<x, y>>; pre-fix counted 121 due to spurious successor that dropped `timestamp' = timestamp + 1`

column1: work_queue
column5: regression for T1.3 wrapper-Next fairness fix; 15003 distinct; pre-fix tlaplusplus reported a false-positive Buchi-style fairness violation on the single-state self-loop SCC at the terminated state

column1: primed_arg_action_staging
column5: regression: an action operator called with a primed argument (Bump(x, x')) must stage x' via param-aliasing (x' = x+1) so a later `seen' = seen \cup {x'}` reads it; pre-fix the compiled/execute_branch action paths value-bound the unstaged prime, x' never staged, Step never fired, exploration froze at 1 state (found investigating MCNanoSmall)

column1: nested_action_constraint
column5: regression: Next == /\ Act /\ Constraint where Act is a nested-disjunction action and Constraint reads a prime (\A i : failed'[i] = ...); in reachable states where Act is disabled the inline empty-fallback must trust the compiled 0-successor result, not propagate the interpreted evaluator's error, else next_states panics (function application unsupported for ModelValue) and stalls at 1 state (found in Chandra-Toueg EnvironmentController)

column1: # KNOWN DIVERGENT SPECS (not gated; tracked for fix in follow-up work)
# These exposed real semantic divergences during T1 (RELEASE_1.0.0_LOG.md
# entry 2026-04-25). They are intentionally excluded so the CI gate stays
# meaningful. Re-add them once the underlying bug is fixed.
# (ViewTest.tla was promoted to the active list above on 2026-04-25 after the
# T1.5 splitter fix; it now reports 106 distinct states matching TLC.)
# (WorkQueue.tla was promoted to the active list above on 2026-04-26 after
# the T1.3 wrapper-Next fairness fix; it now reports no fairness violation
# matching TLC.)
nat_override_membership
column5: regression: x \in (Nat \ {0}) under Nat <- Override must not enumerate infinite Nat (fixes MCLamportMutex/TLCSailfish false-violations)

column1: unchanged_defined_tuple
column5: regression: UNCHANGED of a defined variable-tuple (frozen == <<failed>>) must stage the component prime failed' so a trailing constraint reading failed' resolves, else next_states panics (function application unsupported for ModelValue); found in Chandra-Toueg EnvironmentController's UNCHANGED envVars

column1: exists_guarded_nested_disjunction
column5: regression: \E i : /\ guard /\ (\/ A \/ B) must keep the inner disjunction inside the quantified conjunction; splitting it to top-level disjuncts drops the guard + shared primed conjuncts and fires A/B from every state, reading an unstaged prime (EnvironmentController's ProcTick spurious partial branches)
